title: agalinis linarioides subsp. linarioides
intro: Agalinis linarioides subsp. linarioides, also known as Purple Gerardia, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It belongs to the Orobanchaceae family and is known for its purple flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. Agalinis linarioides subsp. linarioides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to promote health benefits, including relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system.
